vocabulary.meaning: in or to a foreign country
vocabulary.example: He decided to study abroad in Germany next year.

vocabulary.meaning: place such as a room or house for someone to live or stay in
vocabulary.example: The hotel offers comfortable accommodation for tourists.

vocabulary.meaning: reserve a place, ticket, or hotel room in advance
vocabulary.example: We should book our flight tickets early.

vocabulary.meaning: short period of rest or relaxation
vocabulary.example: Let's take a ten-minute break from studying.

vocabulary.meaning: decide that a planned event or reservation will not happen
vocabulary.example: They had to cancel the flight due to bad weather.

vocabulary.meaning: board a train, bus, or plane in time
vocabulary.example: Hurry up if you want to catch the last bus!

vocabulary.meaning: comfortable bus used for carrying passengers on long journeys
vocabulary.example: We traveled to London by coach.

vocabulary.meaning: suitable for your needs and causing no difficulty
vocabulary.example: The supermarket is in a very convenient location.

vocabulary.meaning: hit another vehicle or object violently; an accident involving vehicles
vocabulary.example: The car crashed into a tree, but luckily no one was injured.

vocabulary.meaning: very full of people
vocabulary.example: The train station was crowded with commuters.

vocabulary.meaning: holiday spent traveling on a large ship
vocabulary.example: They went on a cruise around the Mediterranean.

vocabulary.meaning: make someone or something late; period of time you must wait
vocabulary.example: The train was delayed by an hour.

vocabulary.meaning: place to which someone or something is going
vocabulary.example: Paris is a popular tourist destination.

vocabulary.meaning: boat that carries passengers and vehicles across a river or sea
vocabulary.example: We took the ferry to cross the river.

vocabulary.meaning: journey made by flying in an aircraft
vocabulary.example: Our flight to Tokyo was smooth and comfortable.

vocabulary.meaning: belonging to a country other than your own
vocabulary.example: Learning a foreign language opens up many opportunities.

vocabulary.meaning: area of water on the coast where ships can tie up safely
vocabulary.example: Several fishing boats were docked in the harbour.

vocabulary.meaning: act of traveling from one place to another, especially a long distance
vocabulary.example: The journey across the desert took three days.

vocabulary.meaning: bags and suitcases you carry when traveling
vocabulary.example: You must keep your luggage with you at all times.

vocabulary.meaning: not far away; close
vocabulary.example: They live in a nearby town.

vocabulary.meaning: put clothes and items into a bag or suitcase for travel
vocabulary.example: I need to pack my suitcase tonight.

vocabulary.meaning: official document showing your identity for international travel
vocabulary.example: You need a valid passport to travel abroad.

vocabulary.meaning: flat area next to a railway track where passengers get on and off trains
vocabulary.example: The train for Paris leaves from platform 5.

vocabulary.meaning: buses, trains, and other transport open to the general public
vocabulary.example: Using public transport is better for the environment.

vocabulary.meaning: arrive at a place or destination
vocabulary.example: We reached the hotel just before midnight.

vocabulary.meaning: place where many people go for holidays or recreation
vocabulary.example: We stayed at a luxury beach resort.

vocabulary.meaning: something you buy to remember a place you visited
vocabulary.example: She bought a keychain as a souvenir of her trip to Rome.

vocabulary.meaning: vehicles moving on a road or public highway
vocabulary.example: There was a lot of traffic on the highway this morning.

vocabulary.meaning: journey to a place and back again, especially for pleasure or a short time
vocabulary.example: We are planning a weekend trip to the mountains.

vocabulary.meaning: machine used for transporting people or goods, such as a car or truck
vocabulary.example: Tractors and trucks are heavy vehicles.

vocabulary.meaning: acceptable or right for a particular person or situation
vocabulary.example: This hotel is suitable for families with children.

vocabulary.meaning: fitting closely or held firmly
vocabulary.example: Hold my hand tightly so you don't get lost.

vocabulary.meaning: aircraft without wings that flies using overhead blades
vocabulary.example: The rescue team arrived in a helicopter.

vocabulary.meaning: building where trains or buses stop for passengers
vocabulary.example: Meet me at the railway station at 6 p.m.

vocabulary.meaning: man who rides a horse and looks after cattle, especially in the US
vocabulary.example: The cowboy rode his horse across the ranch.

vocabulary.meaning: large animal with four legs and a mane, used for riding
vocabulary.example: He learned to ride a horse when he was a teenager.

vocabulary.meaning: known and recognized by many people
vocabulary.example: The city is famous for its beautiful bridges.

vocabulary.meaning: arrangement to meet someone at a particular time and place
vocabulary.example: I have a doctor's appointment at 10 o'clock.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: enter a car or small vehicle
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: Get into the car, we're leaving now.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: leave a bus, train, plane, or bicycle
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: Make sure you take all your bags when you get off the train.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: board a bus, train, plane, or bicycle
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: I got onto the bus at High Street.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: leave a car, room, building, or area
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: She got out of the taxi and walked into the hotel.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: leave a place or depart on holiday
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: We're going away for the weekend.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: return to a place or person
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: I must go back to the office; I left my phone there.
EX: They set off early in the morning to avoid the traffic.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: leave the ground and start to fly
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: The plane took off on time despite the rain.

prepositionalPhrases.meaning: method of transport used for travel
prepositionalPhrases.example: EX: It is much faster to travel to London by air.

prepositionalPhrases.meaning: on or in a ship, train, or aircraft
prepositionalPhrases.example: EX: There were over three hundred passengers on board the flight.

prepositionalPhrases.meaning: walking rather than riding in a vehicle
prepositionalPhrases.example: EX: The museum is close enough to reach on foot.

prepositionalPhrases.meaning: taking a vacation or period of rest away from work
prepositionalPhrases.example: EX: They are currently on holiday in Spain.

prepositionalPhrases.meaning: happening at the planned or expected time
prepositionalPhrases.example: EX: The train arrived exactly on schedule.

prepositionalPhrases.meaning: located on the land near the sea
prepositionalPhrases.example: EX: They bought a beautiful holiday home on the coast.

wordFormation.meaning: attract: pull or draw interest / attractive: beautiful or pleasant to look at / attraction: interesting place or event that tourists visit
wordFormation.example:
EX: The theme park attracts millions of visitors.
EX: She wore an attractive dress to the party.
EX: The Eiffel Tower is a famous tourist attraction.

wordFormation.meaning: back: rear part of the body or object / backwards: in the direction that is behind you
wordFormation.example:
EX: He wrote his name on the back of the envelope.
EX: She took a step backwards to look at the painting.

wordFormation.meaning: choose / chose / chosen: select from a number of options / choice: act of selecting or the range of options available
wordFormation.example:
EX: You can choose whichever book you like.
EX: He had to make a difficult choice between two jobs.

wordFormation.meaning: comfort: state of physical ease / comfortable: providing physical ease / uncomfortable: not providing ease; causing discomfort
wordFormation.example:
EX: These soft pillows provide a lot of comfort.
EX: The hotel rooms were warm and comfortable.
EX: That wooden chair is very uncomfortable to sit on.

wordFormation.meaning: depart: leave or start a journey / departure: act of leaving a place
wordFormation.example:
EX: The train will depart from platform 3 at 9:00.
EX: Passengers should check the departure screens.

wordFormation.meaning: direct: straight or without stopping / direction: path along which something moves or points
wordFormation.example:
EX: There is a direct flight from Hanoi to Tokyo.
EX: The guide pointed in the direction of the museum.

wordFormation.meaning: drive / drove / driven: operate and guide a vehicle / driver: person who drives a vehicle
wordFormation.example:
EX: My father drives to work every day.
EX: The bus driver was very friendly.

wordFormation.meaning: fly / flew / flown: move through the air / flight: journey made by flying
wordFormation.example:
EX: Birds can fly high in the sky.
EX: We booked a flight to Paris.

wordFormation.meaning: travel: make a journey / traveller: person who travels
wordFormation.example:
EX: They love to travel to foreign countries.
EX: The hotel is popular with business travellers.

wordFormation.meaning: visit: go to see a person or place / visitor: person who visits a person or place
wordFormation.example:
EX: We plan to visit the museum tomorrow.
EX: The museum has a guide to help visitors.

wordPatterns.example: EX: Our house is very close to the station.

wordPatterns.meaning: known by many people for a particular feature or achievement
wordPatterns.example: EX: Paris is famous for its art and cuisine.

wordPatterns.meaning: at a great distance from a place
wordPatterns.example: EX: The hotel is far from the city center.

wordPatterns.meaning: arriving after the planned or expected time
wordPatterns.example: EX: He was late for school this morning.

wordPatterns.meaning: right or acceptable for a particular purpose or person
wordPatterns.example: EX: This movie is not suitable for young children.

wordPatterns.meaning: reach a place at the end of a journey
wordPatterns.example:
EX: We arrived at the station just in time.
EX: They arrived in Tokyo yesterday.

wordPatterns.meaning: politely question someone about a subject
wordPatterns.example: EX: Ask the tourist guide about the local history.
EX: He asked for a glass of water.

wordPatterns.meaning: direct your eyes towards something to see it
wordPatterns.example: EX: Look at the beautiful view from this window.

wordPatterns.meaning: make things ready for a future event
wordPatterns.example: EX: She is preparing for her final exams.

wordPatterns.meaning: give something useful or necessary to someone
wordPatterns.example: EX: The school provides students with textbooks.

wordPatterns.meaning: stay in a place until someone or something arrives
wordPatterns.example: EX: I am waiting for the bus.
